title: libanothamnus occultus subsp. humbertii
intro: Libanothamnus occultus subsp. humbertii, commonly known as Madagascar plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to the island of Madagascar and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Libanothamnus occultus subsp. humbertii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and treat various health conditions.
